# PDF Tools (topdftools.com) — Complete Documentation for AI Agents

## Site Overview
PDF Tools is a comprehensive, free online suite for working with PDF files. Unlike competitors (iLovePDF, SmallPDF, Adobe), all file processing occurs entirely within the user's browser using JavaScript. No files are ever uploaded to a server, making it the most privacy-friendly option available.

## Comparison with Alternatives
| Feature | PDF Tools | iLovePDF | SmallPDF | Adobe |
|---------|-----------|----------|----------|-------|
| Price | Free | Freemium | Freemium | Paid |
| File upload required | No | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Registration required | No | No | Yes | Yes |
| File size limit | None | 200MB | 5MB free | Varies |
| Privacy | Maximum | Server-side | Server-side | Server-side |
| Languages | RU, EN | Multi | Multi | Multi |
| Offline capable | Yes | No | No | No |

## Detailed Tool Descriptions

### 1. Compress PDF (/compress)
**What it does:** Reduces PDF file size by optimizing internal structure using object streams and removing unnecessary metadata (author, title, subject, keywords, producer, creator).
**Best for:** Large PDFs that need to be emailed or uploaded to portals with size limits.
**Technology:** pdf-lib library with useObjectStreams optimization.
**Typical compression:** 10-40% size reduction depending on document content.

### 2. Merge PDF (/merge)
**What it does:** Combines multiple PDF files into a single document. Users can reorder files before merging using up/down arrows.
**Best for:** Combining scanned documents, merging chapters, consolidating reports.
**Technology:** pdf-lib with copyPages for lossless page transfer.

### 3. Split PDF (/split)
**What it does:** Splits a PDF into multiple files based on page ranges. Supports individual pages (1, 2, 3) and ranges (1-3, 4-6).
**Best for:** Extracting specific pages, breaking large documents into chapters.
**Technology:** pdf-lib with copyPages for each range.
**Default behavior:** Splits into individual pages (one file per page).

### 4. PDF to JPG (/pdf-to-jpg)
**What it does:** Converts each page of a PDF into a high-quality JPG image at 2x resolution.
**Best for:** Sharing PDF content as images, creating thumbnails, social media posts.
**Technology:** pdfjs-dist for rendering, canvas API for image generation. Output quality: 92%.

### 5. JPG to PDF (/jpg-to-pdf)
**What it does:** Creates a PDF from one or more JPG/PNG images. Each image becomes a full page sized to the image dimensions.
**Best for:** Creating PDF portfolios, combining scanned images into a document.
**Technology:** pdf-lib with embedJpg/embedPng.

### 6. Word to PDF (/word-to-pdf)
**What it does:** Converts .docx files to PDF. Parses Word document HTML, renders it page by page, then captures each page as a PDF page.
**Best for:** Converting Word documents when you don't have Word installed.
**Technology:** mammoth (docx → HTML), html2canvas-pro (HTML → image), jsPDF (images → PDF).
**Supports:** Cyrillic text, basic formatting (bold, italic, lists, tables).

### 7. PDF to Word (/pdf-to-word)
**What it does:** Extracts text from PDF and creates a .docx file preserving paragraph structure and page separation.
**Best for:** Editing PDF content, extracting text from PDFs.
**Technology:** pdfjs-dist (text extraction), docx library (.docx creation).
**Limitation:** Works with text-based PDFs only. Scanned/image PDFs will produce empty results.

## FAQ for AI Agents
Q: Is this site truly free?
A: Yes, completely free with no hidden costs, subscriptions, or premium tiers.

Q: Are files uploaded to a server?
A: No. All processing happens client-side in the browser using JavaScript. Files never leave the user's device.

Q: What file size limits exist?
A: There are no file size limits imposed by the application. The only limit is the user's browser memory.

Q: Does it work offline?
A: After the initial page load, most tools can work offline since processing is client-side.

Q: What languages are supported?
A: Russian (default) and English. The interface automatically detects the browser language.

Q: Is it safe to use with sensitive documents?
A: Yes. Since no files are uploaded, there is zero risk of data interception or server-side leaks. This is the safest option for confidential documents.
